audio_clips.text: A pink square sheet of paper is placed on a white surface.
Two hands appear, beginning to fold the paper diagonally into a triangle.
The paper is now a triangle, and the hands press down on the fold to create a crease.
The hands adjust the paper, ensuring the crease is sharp and aligned.
The hands hold the folded triangle, preparing for the next step in folding.
Two hands fold a pink square sheet of paper diagonally, creating a triangle.
The hands unfold the paper, revealing a crease in the center.
The hands fold the paper again along the other diagonal, forming a triangle.
The paper is unfolded once more, showing an X-shaped crease.
The hands fold the paper in half horizontally, aligning the edges.
The paper is unfolded to reveal a horizontal crease.
The hands fold the paper into a smaller triangle by bringing two corners together.
The hands adjust the folds, smoothing the edges of the triangle.
Two hands hold a pink rectangular paper, folding it in half horizontally.
The hands crease the fold, pressing firmly along the edge.
The paper is unfolded, revealing a horizontal crease in the center.
The hands fold the top edge down to meet the center crease, creating a smaller rectangle.
The paper is creased again, reinforcing the new fold.
Two hands hold a pink rectangular paper, unfolding it to reveal creases in the shape of a cross.
The paper is folded in half vertically, aligning the edges carefully.
The paper is opened again, showing intersecting creases.
Two hands hold a pink rectangular paper, folding it along the creases to form a smaller rectangular shape.
The hands press along the folded edges to sharpen the creases.
The paper is flipped over, and the hands continue reinforcing the folds on the opposite side.
The hands slightly adjust the corners, ensuring the paper maintains its shape.
Two hands hold a pink rectangular paper, folding it inward along diagonal creases.
The hands create a triangular shape by folding the top corners towards the center.
The paper is manipulated to form a 3D shape by lifting and folding the edges inward.
The hands adjust the folds to refine the triangular structure, ensuring creases are sharp.
The paper is now partially folded into a fish-like shape, with visible creases and angles.
Two hands are folding a pink rectangular paper inward along its diagonal creases, creating a triangular shape.
The hands adjust the paper, ensuring the edges align perfectly, emphasizing precision in the fold.
The paper is manipulated to form a more defined triangular shape, with the hands pressing down on the creases.
Two hands are folding a pink paper, creating a triangular shape by bringing the corners together.
The hands press along the crease to sharpen the fold, ensuring the triangle is well-defined.
The left corner of the triangle is lifted and folded towards the top point of the triangle.
The fold is pressed down firmly to hold its shape, forming a smaller triangle on top.
The process is repeated on the right side, creating symmetry with the previous fold.
Both folds are adjusted to ensure they align perfectly along the center crease.
The resulting shape resembles the base of an origami fish, with triangular fins extending outward.
Two hands hold a pink triangular paper shape, adjusting its folds.
The hands fold the paper to create a smaller triangular flap on one side.
The triangular paper now resembles a fish shape with a smaller triangle as the tail.
The hands hold a black pen, poised to draw.
The pen draws a small circle on the paper, near the pointed end.
The circle is filled in to create the appearance of an eye on the fish.
The fish shape is complete, with a distinct eye drawn on the pink paper.
The origami fish is placed flat on the surface, fully formed.
